steps.title: Identify the true subject
steps.content: The subject of the blank is “an abstract cubist painting.” The intervening phrase about Marcel Duchamp and other works does not change the subject's singular number.

steps.title: Reject agreement errors
steps.content: Choice A is plural, so “painting were entering” is wrong. Choice B is a base/plural present form and does not agree. Choice D is plural present perfect, so “painting have entered” is also wrong.

steps.title: Choose singular present perfect
steps.content: The remaining verb agrees with the singular subject and shows a past action whose result still matters: the painting has become part of the canon. The answer is C.
